LHX.sentence: L3Harris slipped a touch and is trading well off its highs, but nothing has changed about the business — defense spending stays steady, and it's always been the cheaper one of the big military contractors.

ASML.sentence: ASML had a nice pop this week; investors are betting the chipmaking rebound is real, and this is still the only company on earth that makes their kind of chip-printing machine.

MSFT.sentence: No fresh reading on Microsoft today, but the AI-cloud story keeps grinding along quietly in the background.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher drifted down again and now looks genuinely cheap for a company that sells the essential equipment to nearly every biotech and research lab in the world.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge is flat and calm, running its oil and gas pipelines like a toll road, though the income it pays out isn't as generous relative to safer bonds as it usually is.

NOW.sentence: No data on ServiceNow today — the big-company and government demand for their workflow-automation software tends to move slowly anyway.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore ticked up a bit, and while its mining profits are in a deep trough right now, that low point has historically been the moment these diversified miners start looking attractive.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin nudged higher but is still well off its high for the year, sitting near the bottom of its recent range after a long slide.

KOF.sentence: The Mexican Coca-Cola bottler barely moved and looks reasonably priced for a company that dominates soft-drink distribution across Latin America.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras firmed up today and remains a huge dividend payer, though its profits may be near a peak that oil cycles eventually roll over from.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk, the Danish company that hauls containers across the oceans, had a solid day and is trading for less than its own fleet of ships is worth — a classic sign it's cheap.
